Ikulungwane Setup Guide

Beginner-friendly installation, deployment, and system map.

Overview Requirements Local Setup cPanel Deployment Installer Wizard Email Setup Admin Area System Map Routes Database Security Troubleshooting
Project Documentation

Ikulungwane Holdings Website Setup

This document explains how to install, deploy, and manage the Ikulungwane Holdings system. It is written for someone with little to no technical experience, but it also maps the important system parts for future developers.

1. What This System Is

Public Website

The public website is built with React and Vite. It includes pages such as Home, Portfolio, Services, About, Journal, Contact, and Book a Session.

Admin System

The admin area lets the owner manage portfolio items, services, testimonials, journal posts, team members, bookings, leads, page content, branding, and settings.

Installer Wizard

The installer lives at /setup. It collects database details, creates tables, creates the first admin, saves website settings, and locks itself after installation.

cPanel Backend

The backend is PHP inside public_html/api. It handles setup, login, email sending, and secure server-only configuration.

2. What You Need

For Local Work

  • A computer with Windows, macOS, or Linux.
  • Node.js 18 or newer.
  • npm, included with Node.js.
  • Git, if you want to pull or push from GitHub.
  • A code editor such as VS Code.

For cPanel Hosting

  • cPanel hosting account.
  • PHP 8.0 or newer.
  • PDO extension enabled.
  • PDO MySQL driver enabled.
  • MySQL or MariaDB database.
  • Apache rewrite support for .htaccess.
  • cPanel email account if website email is required.
Beginner note: If you are deploying to cPanel, you do not need to install Node.js on the server. You build the site on your computer, then upload the finished dist files to public_html.

3. Local Setup On Your Computer

  1. Open the project folder on your computer.
  2. Open a terminal in the project folder.
  3. Install the project packages:
npm install
  1. Start the development server:
npm run dev
  1. Open the local URL shown in the terminal, usually:
http://127.0.0.1:5173
Important: The local Vite server does not run PHP. The installer page can open locally, but database installation must be tested on a PHP/cPanel server or a local PHP server.

4. Deploy To cPanel

Step A: Build The Website

Run this command on your computer:

npm run build

This creates a folder named dist. That folder contains the final website files.

Step B: Upload To cPanel

  1. Log in to cPanel.
  2. Open File Manager.
  3. Open the public_html folder.
  4. Upload everything inside the dist folder into public_html.
  5. Do not upload the dist folder itself. Upload the files inside it.

Step C: Confirm Important Files Exist

  • public_html/index.html
  • public_html/.htaccess
  • public_html/assets
  • public_html/api/setup.php
  • public_html/api/auth.php
  • public_html/api/send-email.php
  • public_html/api/config/.htaccess

Step D: Create The Database In cPanel

  1. In cPanel, open MySQL Databases.
  2. Create a new database. Example: cpaneluser_ikulungwane.
  3. Create a new database user. Example: cpaneluser_ikuadmin.
  4. Give the user a strong password. Save it somewhere safe for the installer.
  5. Add the user to the database.
  6. Give the user All Privileges.

5. Run The Installer Wizard

After uploading the site to cPanel, open:

https://yourdomain.com/setup
Step Route What It Does
1. Welcome /setup Explains what the installer will do.
2. System Requirements /setup Checks PHP, PDO, PDO MySQL, JSON, sessions, and writable config folder.
3. Database Setup /setup/database Collects database host, port, database name, username, and password. Tests the connection.
4. Admin Account /setup/admin Creates the first super admin account.
5. Website Settings /setup/site Collects site name, logo, phone, email, address, and WhatsApp number.
6. Finish Installation /setup/finish Creates tables, saves settings, marks the system installed, and redirects to admin login.

Database Values The Installer Asks For

  • Database host: usually localhost.
  • Database port: usually 3306.
  • Database name: the name created in cPanel.
  • Database username: the database user created in cPanel.
  • Database password: the password for that database user.
After installation, the system saves installed=true in the system_settings table. Normal visitors cannot use /setup after that.

6. Set Up cPanel Email

The contact and booking forms send email using public_html/api/send-email.php.

  1. In cPanel, open Email Accounts.
  2. Create or select the email account, for example info@yourdomain.com.
  3. Click Connect Devices to see SMTP details.
  4. Open public_html/api in File Manager.
  5. Copy email-config.example.php.
  6. Rename the copy to email-config.php.
  7. Edit email-config.php and add the real SMTP details.
'smtp_host' => 'mail.yourdomain.com',
'smtp_port' => 465,
'smtp_secure' => true,
'smtp_verify_peer' => true,
'smtp_username' => 'info@yourdomain.com',
'smtp_password' => 'your-cpanel-email-password',
'from_name' => 'Ikulungwane Holdings',
'from_email' => 'info@yourdomain.com',
'notification_emails' => ['info@yourdomain.com'],

After saving the file, log in to admin and go to Settings - Email. Enable website email and send a test email.

7. Admin Area

After the installer finishes, it redirects to:

/admin/login

Content

  • Portfolio
  • Services
  • Testimonials
  • Journal
  • Team

Clients

  • Bookings
  • Leads
  • Contact messages

Website

  • Page Builder
  • Logo and favicon
  • Contact details
  • Theme colors
  • Typography

System

  • Email setup
  • Navigation links
  • SEO details
  • Legal footer links

8. System Map

Visitor

Views public pages, submits contact forms, or books a session.

React Frontend

Displays pages, admin UI, page builder, and installer wizard.

PHP API

Runs setup, login, email sending, and server-side database work.

MySQL Database

Stores install flag, admin user, content, bookings, leads, and settings.

Main Flow

Browser -> React app -> PHP API -> MySQL database.

Important Folders

Folder/File Purpose
src React source code for pages, components, admin, and setup wizard.
public Static files copied into the final build.
public/api PHP backend endpoints for cPanel hosting.
public/api/config Protected server config folder. The installer writes database credentials here.
database/mysql MySQL install flag schema reference.
database/postgres Postgres schema reference for future hosted backends.
dist Final build folder created by npm run build. Upload its contents to cPanel.

9. Route Map

Public Routes

  • / - Home
  • /portfolio - Portfolio
  • /services - Services
  • /about - About
  • /blog - Journal
  • /contact - Contact
  • /booking - Book a Session

Setup Routes

  • /setup
  • /setup/database
  • /setup/admin
  • /setup/site
  • /setup/finish

Admin Routes

  • /admin/login
  • /admin
  • /admin/page-builder
  • /admin/portfolio
  • /admin/services
  • /admin/bookings
  • /admin/leads
  • /admin/settings

Backend API Routes

  • /api/setup.php
  • /api/auth.php
  • /api/send-email.php

10. Database Tables

Important for cPanel/phpMyAdmin: use database/mysql/schema.sql. Do not import database/postgres/schema.sql into MariaDB/MySQL. PostgreSQL files contain syntax such as CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto;, which causes MySQL error #1064.

The installer creates the required tables automatically. The most important table is the install flag table:

CREATE TABLE system_settings (
  id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  setting_key VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  setting_value TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

After installation, the system inserts:

INSERT INTO system_settings (setting_key, setting_value)
VALUES ('installed', 'true');

Main Tables

Table Stores
system_settingsInstall flag and core system values.
app_usersAdmin and user accounts with hashed passwords.
site_settingsGlobal website settings.
page_contentPage builder content.
portfolio_projectsPortfolio work.
servicesServices and package information.
testimonialsClient reviews.
blog_postsJournal posts.
team_membersTeam profiles.
bookingsBooking requests.
contact_messagesContact form leads.
email_delivery_logsFuture email delivery tracking.

11. Security Notes

  • Do not put database passwords in React files.
  • Do not store database setup details in localStorage.
  • Use the installer because it sends database details to PHP only.
  • Keep public_html/api/config/database.php protected.
  • Use a strong admin password with at least 8 characters.
  • After installation, /setup is blocked for normal visitors.
  • Only a logged-in super admin should reopen setup tools.
  • Do not commit real email-config.php or database.php files to GitHub.

12. Troubleshooting

/setup shows API not available

You are probably viewing the local Vite server. PHP does not run there. Upload the build to cPanel or run the project behind a PHP server.

Database connection fails

Check database name, username, password, host, and port. On cPanel the database and username often start with your cPanel username.

If you see SQLSTATE[HY000] [1045] Access denied, MySQL rejected the username or password. Copy the exact MySQL username from cPanel, reset the database user's password if needed, then use Add User To Database and choose All Privileges.

Pages 404 after refresh

Make sure .htaccess was uploaded to public_html. Some file managers hide dotfiles by default.

If /admin/login, /setup, or /portfolio shows a cPanel 404 page, upload htaccess.txt to public_html, rename it to .htaccess, and enable Show Hidden Files (dotfiles) in cPanel File Manager.

Email test fails

Check email-config.php. If your host has certificate issues, try setting 'smtp_verify_peer' => false.

Admin login fails

Make sure installation finished successfully and the first admin account was created. Check the app_users table.

Installer says config is not writable

Make sure public_html/api/config exists and is writable by PHP. Ask your host if you are unsure.

Ikulungwane Holdings system documentation. Keep this file with the project for future handovers and hosting support.
